title_en: Al-Mudawwanah's Legacy and the Maliki School in North Africa
title_ar: إرث المدونة والمذهب المالكي في شمال أفريقيا
content_en:
Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra's influence on the development of Islamic legal culture in North Africa, West Africa, and Al-Andalus is among the most significant in Islamic history. Through Sahnun's transmission and teaching in Kairouan, and through the scholars his school produced, Al-Mudawwanah became the foundation on which centuries of Maliki jurisprudence were built.

Sahnun's position as a judge in Kairouan gave Al-Mudawwanah not only academic authority but judicial force. When the qadi of the most important city in North Africa applied the Maliki positions recorded in Al-Mudawwanah to actual cases, those positions became the practical law of the region. Subsequent generations of Maliki judges in North Africa operated within the framework that Sahnun had established, and Al-Mudawwanah remained the primary reference for Maliki judicial practice.

The commentary tradition on Al-Mudawwanah is extensive. The major commentaries — including those of Ibn Rushd al-Jadd (d. 520 AH / 1126 CE) and others — represent generations of North African and Andalusian scholars engaging with its positions, clarifying ambiguities, and extending its rulings to new questions. This commentary tradition is one of the richest in any school of Islamic law and testifies to the foundational status of the text.

In West Africa, where Islam spread through trade routes from North Africa, the Maliki school became established partly because North African Maliki scholars were the primary intermediaries. The legal tradition they brought was rooted in Al-Mudawwanah and its derivatives, and Maliki fiqh has remained the dominant legal tradition of Muslim West Africa — from Senegal and Mali to Nigeria and Sudan — for over a millennium. The contemporary Islamic legal culture of West Africa still reflects this Maliki heritage.

Al-Andalus — Islamic Spain — was another major center of Maliki scholarship. The Maliki scholars of Córdoba, Seville, and Granada produced works of great sophistication that engaged with Al-Mudawwanah's positions in the context of the complex Islamic-Christian border society of the Iberian Peninsula. Ibn Rushd's Bidayat al-Mujtahid, which compares the positions of different schools with particular attention to Maliki fiqh, grew out of this Andalusian Maliki tradition rooted in Al-Mudawwanah.

For contemporary scholars and practitioners of Maliki fiqh, Al-Mudawwanah remains essential reading — not as a practical reference for issuing fatwas, which is better served by later systematic works, but as the source that illuminates the origins and logic of the school's distinctive positions. Reading Al-Mudawwanah is reading Malik's fiqh in its most direct transmission.
title_id: Warisan Al-Mudawwanah dan Mazhab Maliki di Afrika Utara
content_id:
Pengaruh Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra terhadap perkembangan budaya hukum Islam di Afrika Utara, Afrika Barat, dan Andalusia merupakan salah satu yang paling signifikan dalam sejarah Islam. Melalui transmisi dan pengajaran Sahnun di Kairouan, dan melalui murid-murid serta karya-karya yang menyebar dari pusat intelektual tersebut, Al-Mudawwanah menjadi pilar mazhab Maliki di barat dunia Islam.

Sahnun sendiri berhasil menjadikan Kairouan sebagai pusat pembelajaran Maliki yang bersaing dengan Mesir dan Irak. Murid-muridnya menyebarkan Al-Mudawwanah ke seluruh Afrika Utara, dan ketika Andalusia semakin mapan sebagai pusat pembelajaran Islam, mazhab Maliki — dengan Al-Mudawwanah sebagai rujukan utamanya — menjadi mazhab dominan di sana. Para ulama besar Andalusia seperti Ibn Rushd al-Jadd dan Ibn Abd al-Barr semua mengakui hutang budi mereka kepada Al-Mudawwanah.

Pengaruh Al-Mudawwanah di Afrika Barat juga sangat dalam. Melalui jalur perdagangan dan perjalanan haji, mazhab Maliki menyebar ke wilayah-wilayah yang kini menjadi Mali, Senegal, Guinea, dan negara-negara Afrika Barat lainnya. Hingga kini, sebagian besar Muslim Afrika Barat mengikuti mazhab Maliki, dan Al-Mudawwanah masih dipelajari di lembaga-lembaga pendidikan Islam tradisional di wilayah tersebut.

Dalam konteks akademis modern, Al-Mudawwanah dipelajari sebagai salah satu sumber terpenting untuk memahami perkembangan hukum Islam pada abad ke-2 dan ke-3 hijriah. Format tanya-jawabnya memberikan jendela yang unik ke dalam proses pembentukan hukum Islam di masa ketika mazhab-mazhab masih dalam proses konsolidasi, dan posisi-posisi yang tersimpan di dalamnya menjadi data berharga bagi para peneliti sejarah hukum Islam.
content_ar:
تُشكّل المدونة الكبرى الأساس الذي بُني عليه الفقه المالكي في المغرب والأندلس وأفريقيا حتى يومنا. وقد لقيت من علماء المالكية اهتماماً شارحاً واسعاً: فمن أبرز شروحها "التهذيب في اختصار المدونة" للبرادعي و"المقدمات الممهدات" لابن رشد الجد.

ويصف علماء المالكية المدونة بأنها بعد القرآن والسنة الركن الأساسي للمذهب ولا يخلو عالم مالكي من حفظ مسائلها الكبرى ومعرفة ما فيها من اجتهادات. وقد صار التفقه في المدونة شرطاً ضمنياً للتخصص في الفقه المالكي في المغرب والأندلس إلى أن ظهرت المختصرات.

والقيمة الدائمة للمدونة أنها تُتيح لمن يقرأها الاطلاع على المذهب المالكي في صورته الأولى الحيّة قبل أن يتصلّب في مواقف مستقرة. وهذا يُعطي الباحث فهماً تاريخياً وعمقاً منهجياً لا يُتاح من خلال المختصرات والمتون المتأخرة وحدها.
